skill.name: python-patterns
skill.description: Python development principles and decision-making. Framework selection, async patterns, type hints, project structure. Teaches thinking, not copying.
skill.summary: A decision-making framework for Python development covering framework selection, async patterns, type hints, and project architecture for 2025.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 41 static analysis findings were determined to be false positives. The external_commands detections (35 locations) incorrectly flagged markdown code blocks (```) as shell execution - these are documentation examples. The network finding is a test URL in example code. The blocker patterns are text resembling security issues but are educational content. This is a safe educational skill teaching Python development decision-making with no executable code or security risks.

content.value_statement: Stop copying code patterns without understanding. Learn to make informed Python architecture decisions based on your specific project context, framework needs, and team requirements.
actual_capabilities: Guide framework selection based on project requirements (API, full-stack, microservices)
Explain async vs sync decision criteria with library recommendations
Provide type hinting strategies for functions, classes, and APIs
Recommend project structures for small scripts to large applications
Teach error handling patterns for FastAPI and Django
Explain background task solutions from simple to distributed workflows
limitations: Does not provide ready-to-copy production code implementations
Requires user to understand their specific project context
Focuses on Python web development patterns, not data science or ML workflows

use_cases.title: New Python web project setup
use_cases.description: A developer starting a new API project needs guidance on choosing between FastAPI, Django, and Flask based on their requirements for admin interfaces, async support, and team familiarity.

use_cases.title: Async migration planning
use_cases.description: A team with an existing sync codebase needs to understand when async provides benefits versus when it adds unnecessary complexity to their application.

use_cases.title: Code review and best practices
use_cases.description: A developer reviewing team code needs reference patterns for proper error handling, type annotations, and project organization decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Framework Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to build a [type of application]. My team has experience with [frameworks]. We need [features like admin, async, ORM]. Which Python framework should I choose and why?
prompt_templates.scenario: Choosing the right framework for a new project

prompt_templates.title: Async Decision Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: My application does [describe operations: database calls, CPU processing, HTTP requests]. Should I use async or sync Python? Explain the tradeoffs for my specific case.
prompt_templates.scenario: Deciding between async and synchronous code patterns

prompt_templates.title: Project Structure Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Here is my current project structure: [paste structure]. I am building [application type] with [framework]. Suggest improvements based on best practices for maintainability and testing.

prompt_templates.title: Error Handling Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: I am building a [FastAPI/Django] application that needs consistent error responses. Show me the pattern for custom exceptions, exception handlers, and error response format that hides internal details from clients.

output_examples.input: I need to build an API for a mobile app with user authentication and real-time notifications. My team knows JavaScript but is new to Python.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: FastAPI. Reasoning: FastAPI provides native async support for real-time features, automatic API documentation helpful for JavaScript developers transitioning to Python, Pydantic for clear request/response validation, and a gentle learning curve. Consider adding WebSockets for notifications and SQLAlchemy async for database operations.

output_examples.input: Should I convert my Flask app to async? It mainly processes uploaded files and generates reports.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: Keep it synchronous. Reasoning: File processing and report generation are CPU-bound operations, not I/O-bound. Async provides benefits for waiting on external services (database, HTTP), not computation. Converting to async would add complexity without performance gains. Consider multiprocessing for parallel file processing instead.

best_practices: Always ask clarifying questions about project context before recommending frameworks or patterns
Separate concerns consistently: routes handle HTTP, services contain business logic, repositories manage data access
Use type hints for all public APIs and function signatures to improve code clarity and catch errors early
anti_patterns: Defaulting to the same framework for every project without considering requirements
Mixing synchronous and asynchronous code without understanding the implications
Putting business logic directly in route handlers instead of service layers

faq.question: When should I choose FastAPI over Django?
faq.answer: Choose FastAPI for API-first projects, microservices, or when you need async support and modern Python features. Choose Django when you need a batteries-included solution with built-in admin, ORM, authentication, and templating for full-stack web applications.

faq.question: Is async always better than sync in Python?
faq.answer: No. Async is better for I/O-bound operations like database queries, HTTP calls, and file operations with many concurrent connections. Sync is better for CPU-bound operations, simple scripts, or when your team lacks async experience. The wrong choice can hurt performance.

faq.question: Are type hints required in Python?
faq.answer: Type hints are optional at runtime but highly recommended for public APIs, library code, and team projects. They improve code readability, enable IDE autocomplete, and help catch errors before runtime. Skip them for quick scripts or internal test code.

faq.question: What background task solution should I use?
faq.answer: Use FastAPI BackgroundTasks for simple fire-and-forget operations in the same process. Use Celery, ARQ, or Dramatiq for distributed workers, retry logic, persistent queues, or long-running tasks that need to survive application restarts.

faq.question: How do I handle errors consistently in FastAPI?
faq.answer: Create custom exception classes for your domain errors, register exception handlers that return consistent JSON format with error codes and messages, log internal details server-side, and never expose stack traces to clients for security.

faq.question: What is the recommended project structure for a FastAPI application?
faq.answer: Organize by layers: routes for endpoints, services for business logic, models for database schemas, schemas for Pydantic models, and dependencies for shared resources. For larger projects, consider organizing by feature domain instead of technical layer.
